text: I feel completely hopeless and I don't see a point in continuing anymore.
I've been crying every day for the past two weeks and I don't even know why.
I feel like a complete failure. I've let everyone down including myself.
I'm so exhausted that I can't get out of bed. Everything feels impossible.
I feel completely alone even when I'm surrounded by people. Nobody really knows me.
I've been having dark thoughts lately and I'm scared of myself.
I lost someone very close to me last month and I can't stop grieving.
I feel like I'm disappearing. Like I'm fading away and no one would notice.
Every morning I wake up and the first thought I have is dread. I'm so tired.
I worked so hard for this degree and now I feel like it means nothing.
I'm so anxious about my thesis defense next week I can't sleep at all.
I keep catastrophizing about failing my qualifying exams and it's paralyzing me.
I feel overwhelmed by everything I need to do and I don't know where to start.
My heart races every time I think about presenting in front of my department.
I'm terrified of making a mistake in my research and having everyone find out I'm not smart enough.
I can't concentrate on anything. My mind just races and I can't calm it down.
I've been avoiding my advisor because I'm scared of what they'll say about my progress.
I'm constantly worried I'm behind compared to other students in my cohort.
I feel confused and lost about my research direction and I don't want to admit it.
The uncertainty about my future career is eating me alive every single day.
My advisor rejected my work again without even reading it properly.
I've been waiting three months for feedback and my professor still hasn't responded.
I'm so angry at myself for wasting an entire year on a dead-end research direction.
The department keeps changing requirements and now I have to redo work I already finished.
My labmate keeps taking credit for my ideas and no one seems to notice.
I'm so sick of being told my writing isn't good enough when no one will explain how to improve it.
I got passed over for a fellowship I clearly deserved and I'm furious about it.
My experiment failed for the fourth time and I can't figure out what I'm doing wrong.
I'm annoyed that my department offers no real mental health resources for grad students.
I feel like I'm doing all the work on this collaboration and my co-author does nothing.
Can you give me some tips on how to structure a literature review?
What is the difference between qualitative and quantitative research methods?
How many hours per week do most PhD students typically spend on their research?
I'm looking for resources about time management strategies for graduate school.
What should I include in a research proposal for a funding application?
I'm trying to decide between two research topics. Can you help me think through the tradeoffs?
How do other grad students usually handle the transition from coursework to independent research?
What are some good ways to network at academic conferences?
I want to understand how to write a better abstract for my paper.
Is it normal to feel uncertain about your research direction in the second year?
notes: normalizing question — borderline but phrased neutrally

text: I finally finished my dissertation chapter and my advisor loved it!
I got my first paper accepted and I'm feeling really proud of myself.
Things have been really hard lately but I feel like I'm finally turning a corner.
I found a coping strategy that actually helps with my anxiety and I wanted to share it.
My advisor gave me really encouraging feedback today and I feel motivated again.
I'm excited about a new collaboration that could really push my research forward.
I've been working on my mental health this semester and I can feel the difference.
I passed my qualifying exam and I feel relieved and grateful for everyone who helped me.
I found a community of other grad students who understand what I'm going through.
I'm starting to see the light at the end of the tunnel with my dissertation.
